libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: Set JGroups tests to local networking: use system properties jgroups.bind_addr=127.0.0.1 and java.net.preferIPv4Stack=true for JVM and native test runs. Prefer SHARED_LOOPBACK/SHARED_LOOPBACK_PING stacks over UDP/TCP multicast for channel tests. Set logging properties before LogFactory is first loaded; use jgroups.use.jdk_logger=true for most tests, or define a test custom Log implementation and set jgroups.log_class when deliberately exercising custom logger loading. Avoid external services: JDBC_PING can be covered with an in-test fake java.sql.Driver rather than a database container.

deterministic_setup.reason: JGroups declares SLF4J as an optional dependency and LogFactory detects org.slf4j.Logger via Class.forName; adding it lets tests exercise the optional logging path used by normal deployments.
JGroups SslContextFactory probes org.wildfly.openssl.OpenSSLProvider and org.wildfly.openssl.SSL reflectively; adding the optional provider enables SSL provider detection coverage.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8866 [Automation] native-image run fails for org.jgroups:jgroups:5.2.2.Final; label=fails-native-image-run; body_chars=8015

risks: Default UDP/TCP or multicast stacks may be flaky or hang in CI/native-image runs; keep generated tests loopback-only unless explicitly testing transport setup.
LogFactory and SslContextFactory do static classpath/property detection, so tests must set properties before those classes are initialized.
WildFly OpenSSL is optional and may fall back to the JDK SSL provider depending on native availability; tests should accept either provider state unless specifically validating OpenSSL.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: JGroups core coverage can be local-only, but meaningful existing coverage relies on loopback network properties and optional dynamically loaded logging/SSL integrations.
